title: South Africa vs Canada Lineups: Team News, Injuries, Suspensions and Key Players for World Cup 2026 | MEXC News
original_text:
South Africa vs Canada Lineups: Team News, Injuries, Suspensions and Key Players for World Cup 2026
For feedback or concerns regarding this content, please contact us at crypto.news@mexc.com
South Africa vs Canada is the opening Round of 32 match at the FIFA World Cup 2026, and the team news gives this knockout tie extra intrigue. Both teams are entering unfamiliar territory: Canada have reached the World Cup knockout stage for the first time, while South Africa have also advanced beyond the group stage for the first time.
The match will be played at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Los Angeles, on Sunday, June 28, 2026, with kickoff at 12:00 PM local time. The winner will move into the Round of 16.
This article covers the latest South Africa vs Canada lineups, possible starting XIs, injury updates, suspensions, key players and tactical roles. It does not predict the final score.
Match: South Africa vs Canada
Competition: FIFA World Cup 2026
Round: Round of 32
Venue: SoFi Stadium, Inglewood, Los Angeles
Kickoff: Sunday, June 28, 2026, 12:00 PM PDT
South Africa time: Sunday, June 28, 2026, 9:00 PM SAST
Canada Eastern time: Sunday, June 28, 2026, 3:00 PM EDT
South Africa vs Canada Team News
South Africa and Canada both arrive with major historical momentum, but their squad situations are different.
Reuters reported that Canada’s biggest storyline is the possible return of captain Alphonso Davies, who missed the group stage because of injury. Canada are also monitoring the fitness of Ismaël Koné and Moïse Bombito.
For South Africa, Teboho Mokoena is expected to return after suspension. That is a major boost in midfield. However, Themba Zwane remains suspended, removing one of South Africa’s most experienced creative players.
The key team news is clear:
Canada: Alphonso Davies could return; Ismaël Koné and Moïse Bombito are fitness concerns.
South Africa: Teboho Mokoena returns from suspension; Themba Zwane remains suspended.
Possible Canada Lineup vs South Africa
Canada’s possible lineup depends heavily on Alphonso Davies’ fitness. If Davies is available, he could return either as a left wing-back, left-back or advanced wide player. If he is not ready to start, Canada may choose a more cautious setup.
A possible Canada XI could look like this:
Goalkeeper: Maxime Crépeau
Defenders: Alistair Johnston, Moïse Bombito, Derek Cornelius, Alphonso Davies
Midfielders: Stephen Eustáquio, Ismaël Koné, Jonathan Osorio
Forwards: Tajon Buchanan, Jonathan David, Tani Oluwaseyi
If Davies does not start, Canada could use Richie Laryea or another wide defender to balance the left side. If Bombito is not fit, Canada may need to adjust the central defence and protect the back line with a deeper midfield screen.
Canada’s most important attacking players are likely to be Jonathan David, Tajon Buchanan and Tani Oluwaseyi. David offers movement and finishing, Buchanan gives direct running, and Oluwaseyi can stretch defenders with pace and energy.
Possible South Africa Lineup vs Canada
South Africa’s possible lineup should be shaped by Teboho Mokoena’s return and Themba Zwane’s suspension.
A possible South Africa XI could look like this:
Goalkeeper: Ronwen Williams
Defenders: Khuliso Mudau, Mothobi Mvala, Siyanda Xulu, Aubrey Modiba
Midfielders: Teboho Mokoena, Sphephelo Sithole, Relebohile Mofokeng
Forwards: Oswin Appollis, Evidence Makgopa, Lyle Foster
South Africa may choose a compact structure with quick transitions. The return of Mokoena gives the midfield more control, passing range and set-piece threat. Without Zwane, South Africa may need more creativity from younger attacking players and wide runners.
Ronwen Williams remains one of the most important figures in goal. His leadership and shot-stopping could be decisive in a knockout match where small margins matter.
Canada Injury Updates
Canada’s biggest injury question is Alphonso Davies.
Davies is Canada’s captain and most globally recognized player. If fit, he changes the way Canada can attack and defend. His speed on the left side can create overloads, carry the ball through pressure and force South Africa’s right side to defend deeper.
Canada are also watching Ismaël Koné and Moïse Bombito.
Koné is important because he gives Canada midfield athleticism and ball progression. If he is not fully fit, Canada may need a more conservative midfield setup.
Bombito is important because his recovery pace can help Canada defend counterattacks. If he is limited, South Africa may look to attack space behind Canada’s centre-backs.
Canada’s fitness situation could influence whether they choose a back four, a back three, or a hybrid shape that changes during the match.
South Africa Suspension Updates
South Africa’s key suspension news is mixed.
Teboho Mokoena is available again after serving his suspension. His return is important because he can control tempo, break up play and offer long-range passing from midfield.
Themba Zwane remains suspended. That is a major loss because Zwane is one of South Africa’s most intelligent attacking players. He can operate between the lines, combine in tight spaces and give the team creativity in possession.
Without Zwane, South Africa may need to rely more on:
wide attacks
quick transitions
set pieces
direct forward runs
Mokoena’s passing range
counterattacks into space
This could make South Africa more vertical and less possession-based than they would be with Zwane available.
Key Players for Canada
Alphonso Davies
If Alphonso Davies returns, he becomes Canada’s most important player. His pace, dribbling and ability to play multiple roles make him central to Canada’s game plan.
Davies can create danger from deep positions, but he can also defend against wide transitions. His fitness will be one of the biggest lineup questions before kickoff.
Jonathan David
Jonathan David is Canada’s main finishing threat. His movement between centre-backs, ability to press and calmness in the penalty area make him vital in a knockout match.
Canada will need David to be efficient because knockout games often offer fewer clear chances.
Tajon Buchanan
Tajon Buchanan gives Canada width, speed and one-v-one attacking threat. If South Africa defend narrow, Buchanan can stretch the game and create space for David and the midfield runners.
Stephen Eustáquio
Stephen Eustáquio is important for control. He can help Canada manage possession, switch play and organize the midfield under pressure.
Key Players for South Africa
Teboho Mokoena
Teboho Mokoena’s return is one of South Africa’s most important team-news boosts. He gives the midfield more structure and can influence the match with passing, tackling and set pieces.
Against Canada’s athletic midfield, Mokoena’s decision-making will be crucial.
Ronwen Williams
Ronwen Williams is South Africa’s captain and goalkeeper. In knockout football, goalkeepers can become decisive, especially if the match is tight or reaches extra time.
His communication with the back line will be important against Canada’s pace.
Evidence Makgopa
Evidence Makgopa can give South Africa a focal point in attack. If Canada press high, Makgopa’s ability to hold the ball and bring runners into play may become important.
Relebohile Mofokeng
Relebohile Mofokeng can offer creativity and direct attacking energy. With Themba Zwane suspended, South Africa may need younger attacking players to take more responsibility in the final third.
Tactical Roles to Watch
Canada’s Left Side
Canada’s left side depends on Davies. If he starts, Canada can create overloads and push South Africa deeper. If he is only fit enough for the bench, Canada may become more balanced but less explosive.
South Africa’s Midfield Control
Mokoena’s return can help South Africa compete in midfield. His role will be especially important against Eustáquio and Koné, if Koné is fit enough to start.
Canada’s Pressing Game
Canada may try to press South Africa early and force turnovers. This approach worked well during parts of their group-stage campaign, especially when their forwards and midfielders pressed together.
South Africa’s Transitions
South Africa may look for quick transitions after winning the ball. Without Zwane, they may rely more on direct passing, wide outlets and forward runs rather than long spells of controlled possession.
Set Pieces
Set pieces could be important for both teams. South Africa have aerial targets and Mokoena’s delivery, while Canada have physical defenders and attackers who can threaten from corners and free kicks.
Captain, Goalkeeper and Attacking Options
For Canada, Alphonso Davies is the captain if fit and available. Maxime Crépeau is expected to be a leading goalkeeper option, while Jonathan David is the central attacking reference.
Canada’s attacking options include David, Buchanan, Oluwaseyi and Cyle Larin. The final selection may depend on whether Canada want more pace, more hold-up play or more pressing intensity.
For South Africa, Ronwen Williams remains a major leadership figure from goal. Mokoena’s return strengthens the midfield, while Makgopa, Appollis, Foster and Mofokeng can all offer different attacking profiles.
South Africa’s biggest challenge is replacing Zwane’s creativity. Canada’s biggest challenge is managing key fitness questions before kickoff.
Confirmed Lineups: When Will They Be Released?
Official confirmed lineups are usually released around one hour before kickoff.
Until then, all starting XIs should be treated as possible lineups, not confirmed lineups. Coaches may change shape depending on late fitness tests, tactical plans and matchday conditions.
Fans should check official team channels, FIFA match pages and broadcaster updates before kickoff for the final confirmed South Africa vs Canada lineups.

Final Thoughts
South Africa vs Canada is a historic Round of 32 match because both teams are playing in the World Cup knockout stage for the first time.
Canada’s lineup depends heavily on the fitness of Alphonso Davies, Ismaël Koné and Moïse Bombito. If Davies returns, Canada gain speed and leadership on the left side.
South Africa receive a major boost with Teboho Mokoena returning from suspension, but they must play without Themba Zwane. That could change how they create chances and move the ball in the attacking third.
The final confirmed lineups will arrive close to kickoff, but the key themes are already clear: Canada’s fitness, South Africa’s midfield balance, Davies’ possible return, Mokoena’s availability and Zwane’s absence.
FAQ
What is the latest South Africa vs Canada team news?
Canada are waiting on the fitness of Alphonso Davies, Ismaël Koné and Moïse Bombito. South Africa have Teboho Mokoena back from suspension, while Themba Zwane remains suspended.
Will Alphonso Davies play against South Africa?
Davies could return, but his final availability depends on fitness. Canada’s lineup may change significantly depending on whether he starts, comes off the bench or misses the match.
Is Teboho Mokoena available for South Africa?
Yes. Teboho Mokoena is expected to return after suspension, giving South Africa an important midfield boost.
Is Themba Zwane suspended?
Yes. Themba Zwane remains suspended for South Africa vs Canada.
Who are Canada’s key players?
Canada’s key players include Alphonso Davies, Jonathan David, Tajon Buchanan, Stephen Eustáquio, Ismaël Koné and Moïse Bombito.
Who are South Africa’s key players?
South Africa’s key players include Ronwen Williams, Teboho Mokoena, Evidence Makgopa, Relebohile Mofokeng and Lyle Foster.
When will confirmed lineups be released?
Confirmed lineups are usually released around one hour before kickoff.
Where is South Africa vs Canada being played?
The match will be played at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Los Angeles.
What round is South Africa vs Canada?
South Africa vs Canada is a FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 match.
Where can I read more match context?
Readers can visit South Africa vs Canada World Cup 2026 Prediction: Tactical Breakdown and Match Forecast for broader match context.
